meta.note: Pre-market Monday pull — US-listed cash equities (TLT, EWY, semis, financials) still reflect Friday Jul 3's close since the cash session hasn't opened. FX, rates, gold, VIX futures, and Asia equities are live: USDJPY broke above the 162.00 BOJ threshold overnight, USDKRW round-tripped back above the 1,530 Korea-gate line, and KOSPI gave back part of Friday's rebound. || 17:05 CET structural update: added 2Y breakeven (1.95%, manual/Bloomberg USGGBE02 basis) and explicit 2Y real yield (2.18%); regime reclassified from Inflation Reacceleration to Policy-Tight / Growth-Scare / Disinflation-Pricing; duration score rationale rewritten around the real-rate tightening mechanism.

liquidity.label: No new H.4.1 print yet — TGA/Reserves flat, SOFR eased 2bp below IORB
decomp: TGA holds at $880.2B, no new weekly print (next due Jul 9)
Reserves flat at $2.967T, no new weekly print
RRP flat at $2.18B — still historically low
SOFR eased to 3.64%, now 1bp below IORB (3.65%) — funding stress easing slightly
liquidity.trend_note: TGA +$38B/wk, reserves below $3T and falling — structural drain accelerating

credit.label: Stable — OAS tightened to 2.74%
credit.trend_note: HY OAS tightened from 2.83% to 2.74%, ALLY/CACC holding — credit conditions easing at the margin

inflation.label: Disinflation priced — 2Y BE 1.95% below Fed target
decomp: 2Y breakeven 1.95% — collapsed 143bp since Mar, below 2% target
Real yield rising = headwind for growth
inflation.trend_note: 2Y BE 1.95% (below target), 10Y BE 2.24% anchored, oil flat — inflation expectations fully repriced away. Rising nominals = policy/funding pressure, not inflation. Net effect: real yields rising.

vol.label: Low — VIX 15.84, MOVE 65.76
vol.trend_note: VIX 15.84, MOVE 65.76 — vol receding despite yield move, suggests equity-specific not macro fear

bias: Regime reclassified: 2Y breakeven has collapsed 3.38%→1.95% since March (−143bp, now below the Fed's 2% target on a market-implied basis) while nominal 2Y sits sticky at 4.127% — so the 2Y real yield is ~2.18% and rising. That is passive real-rate tightening into disinflation, not inflation reacceleration. Worst mix for long-duration equities; someone turned off the oxygen and blamed sentiment. Credit is the offset (HY OAS 2.74%, tightening) — this is pressure, not crisis. Watch signal: nominal 2Y rolling over = transition to cuts/growth-scare pricing = tactical risk relief IF credit holds. It is not rolling over yet. Korea gate 1/3 — no Korea adds. Hold semis with tight sizing; do not add duration-sensitive risk into rising real rates.

counterargs.thesis: The weekend FX/Asia moves (USDJPY above 162, USDKRW above 1,530, KOSPI pullback) are a genuine regime shift, not just pre-market noise
supporting: HY OAS tightened to 2.74% — credit calm
counterargs.read: Today has two competing narratives: credit and FX are improving (HY OAS tighter, KRW stronger), but rates are moving against the duration thesis (10Y +4.5bps, 30Y crossing 5%). The Korea gate remains closed because KOSPI failed — FX condition finally opened for the first time but the market condition regressed. Net: cautiously constructive on semis, not yet actionable on Korea. Overlay (Jul 7 PM reclassification): the 2Y breakeven collapse to 1.95% reframes rising nominals as real-rate tightening into disinflation — the inflection to watch is nominal 2Y breaking lower, which would signal cuts/growth-scare pricing and possible tactical relief if credit stays calm.

trade_table.rationale: DRAM +6.81%, CRDO +9.77%, SOXX +2.68%. Recovery from last week's selloff. EWY gate still closed but US semis intact. Conviction maintained.
10Y 4.485% and 30Y 5.003% moving wrong direction. Real yield rising. Duration thesis under pressure from rate move today.
Gate still closed 1/3. USDKRW finally passed (1,511 < 1,530) but KOSPI failed (7,656 < 8,200). EWY SMA20 not yet confirmed. Need all 3 conditions simultaneously.
ALLY/CACC/COF unrevised pre-market at Friday's stabilized levels. HY OAS steady at 2.75%.
USDJPY broke above 162.00 and DXY firmed +0.24% overnight — the dollar-weakness case needs a rethink if this holds into the session.
HYG and OAS unrevised pre-market at 2.75%. No fresh stress signal.
Jul-Sep reserve-drain window still ahead structurally; no new H.4.1 print to offset it this week.

catalysts.event: US cash equities open (today, pre-market pull)
catalysts.note: This pull reflects Friday's close for US names. Revisit once the 9:30am ET open prints live prices for EWY, semis, and financials.
Next scheduled TGA/Reserves print, plus 10Y auction bid-to-cover as a demand signal at these yields.
PCE-peak narrative confirmed or reversed. Hot print = duration selloff risk.
APH $165/$170 spread (APH $164.59) sits right at the lower strike. DRAM and EOSE spreads remain deep OTM.
Quarterly Refunding Announcement — auction sizes and TGA guidance for Q3.

duration.real_2y_note: 2Y real = US02Y 4.127% − 2Y BE 1.95% = 2.18% and RISING as breakevens collapse against a sticky nominal front end. This is the actual pain trade: real-rate tightening into disinflation.

us10y.note: 4.485% and rising — wrong direction for duration thesis. Up from ~4.37% prior session.

us30y.note: Crossed 5.00% — term premium expanding. Long end pricing something beyond just policy.

us02y.note: 4.127% — sticky. Not repricing down despite 2Y breakeven collapsing to 1.95%. The stickiness IS the signal: policy/funding pressure, not inflation fear. WATCH: nominal 2Y falling = transition to cuts/growth-scare pricing = potential risk relief (credit-dependent).

spread_2s10s.note: 0.358% — curve steepening as long end moves more than front. Not inversion risk.

real_2y.note: 2.18% and rising. Nominal 2Y sticky at 4.127% while 2Y breakeven collapsed 3.38%→1.95% since March — passive real-rate tightening. Fed/TGA/funding pressure holding the front end up as inflation expectations reprice away.

changed_today.delta: ↑ rising — real-rate tightening, the actual pain trade

vix.note: VIX 15.84 — still low. Rising slightly alongside yield move. Not a stress signal yet.

oil.note: Oil stable near $69. Hormuz reopening narrative holding. No new inflation impulse from energy.

move.note: MOVE ticking up alongside 10Y/30Y yield rise. Still well below 100 stress threshold. Bond market not in distress.

duration_score.reason: 2Y breakeven has collapsed 3.38%→1.95% since March (−143bp) while nominal 2Y is sticky at 4.127% — so 2Y real yield is 2.18% and RISING passively. This is not inflation reacceleration and not a benign term-premium story: it is real-rate tightening into a disinflationary environment, the worst mix for long-duration equities. Relief trigger: nominal 2Y starts falling (Fed pressure → cuts/growth-scare pricing). It is not falling yet.
duration_score.watch_signal: Nominal 2Y < ~4.00% with credit stable = regime transition to cuts/growth-scare → tactical risk relief. Nominal 2Y < 4.00% with HY OAS widening = growth scare confirmed → no relief.

liquidity_expansion.characteristics: Falling real rates, improving credit, weaker dollar
liquidity_expansion.match: Partial — credit improving, dollar soft, but real rates RISING

disinflationary_slowdown.characteristics: Falling long yields, sticky front end, slowing growth
disinflationary_slowdown.match: Strong — 2Y BE collapsed to 1.95% (below Fed target), sticky front end, growth repricing. Long yields not falling YET because term premium/funding offsets.

recession.characteristics: Falling yields, widening credit spreads, falling earnings
recession.match: No — credit TIGHTENING, yields rising, no earnings collapse

inflation_reacceleration.characteristics: Rising yields, rising breakevens, stronger commodities
inflation_reacceleration.match: Rejected — 2Y breakeven at 1.95% (−143bp since Mar) says inflation expectations are being priced AWAY, not back. Rising nominals are policy/funding pressure, not CPI fear.

liquidity_shock.characteristics: Rising yields, widening credit spreads, dollar strength
liquidity_shock.match: Partial — sticky front end consistent with TGA/funding pressure, but credit tightening (not widening) and dollar flat.

sector_rotation.confidence: Medium — 2Y breakeven collapse resolves the earlier mixed read; front-end stickiness is the remaining open question

actionable_output.structural_liquidity: Neutral — TGA drain ongoing but credit calm
actionable_output.short_term_conditions: Improving — HY OAS tighter, USDKRW broke below 1,530
actionable_output.macro_trend: Real-Rate Tightening: disinflation priced (2Y BE 1.95%) against a sticky, policy-pressured front end
actionable_output.likely_rotation: Rising real rates into disinflation = de-risk high-duration growth; this is NOT the buy-every-semi-dip regime. Quality/cash-flow-now over duration until nominal 2Y breaks lower.
actionable_output.next_catalyst: 10Y Auction Jul 9; then watch nominal 2Y — a break lower flips regime to cuts/growth-scare

confidence_meta.reason: 1 catalyst(s) within 48h (US cash equities open (today, pre-market pull)) — regime call has elevated uncertainty until results are known

structured_conclusion.likely_rotation: De-risk long-duration growth; quality/value over duration until nominal 2Y falls
